title: sempervivum × fauconnettii var. flavipilum
intro: Sempervivum × fauconnettii var. flavipilum, commonly known as the Golden Rooster Sempervivum, is a succulent plant species that belongs to the family Crassulaceae. It is a hybrid of Sempervivum fauconnetii, native to Europe, and Sempervivum tectorum, a type of hens-and-chicks. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues and wounds. The Golden Rooster Sempervivum can be prepared as an herbal remedy through infusions, tinctures, and salves, offering potential health benefits such as soothing coughs, promoting wound healing, and supporting immune function.
